records.ety: [L. consultare, fr. consulere to consult: cf. f. consulter. Cf. Counsel.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Consulted; p. pr. & vb. n. Consulting.]

defs: To seek the opinion or advice of another; to take counsel; to deliberate together; to confer.
quotes: Let us consult upon to-morrow's business.
All the laws of England have been made by the kings England, consulting with the nobility and commons.

defs: To ask advice of; to seek the opinion of; to apply to for information or instruction; to refer to; as, to consult a physician; to consult a dictionary.
quotes: Men forgot, or feared, to consult nature . . . ; they were content to consult libraries.

defs: To have reference to, in judging or acting; to have regard to; to consider; as, to consult one's wishes.
quotes: We are . . . to consult the necessities of life, rather than matters of ornament and delight.

defs: To deliberate upon; to take for.
quotes: Manythings were there consulted for the future, yet nothing was positively resolved.

defs: To bring about by counsel or contrivance; to devise; to contrive.
quotes: Thou hast consulted shame to thy house by cutting off many people.

defs: The act of consulting or deliberating; consultation; also, the result of consulation; determination; decision.
quotes: The council broke; And all grave consults dissolved in smoke.

defs: A council; a meeting for consultation.
